claim_verified: La these AEGIS propose une QUATRIEME implementation delta3 via validate_output + AllowedOutputSpec, apres CaMeL (P081), AgentSpec (P082) et RAGShield (P066).

checks.title: Unregulated large language models produce medical device

candidates.title: LlamaFirewall: An open source guardrail system for building secure AI agents
candidates.abstract_verbatim: Large language models (LLMs) have evolved from simple chatbots into autonomous agents capable of performing complex tasks such as editing production code, orchestrating workflows, and taking higher-stakes actions based on untrusted inputs like webpages and emails. These capabilities introduce new security risks that existing security measures, such as model fine-tuning or chatbot-focused guardrails, do not fully address. Given the higher stakes and the absence of deterministic solutions to mitigate these risks, there is a critical need for a real-time guardrail monitor to serve as a final layer of defense, and support system level, use case specific safety policy definition and enforcement. We introduce LlamaFirewall, an open-source security focused guardrail framework designed to serve as a final layer of defense against security risks associated with AI Agents. Our framework mitigates risks such as prompt injection, agent misalignment, and insecure code risks through three powerful guardrails: PromptGuard 2, a universal jailbreak detector that demonstrates clear state of the art performance; Agent Alignment Checks, a chain-of-thought auditor that inspects agent reasoning for prompt injection and goal misalignment, which, while still experimental, shows stronger efficacy at preventing indirect injections in general scenarios than previously proposed approaches; and CodeShield, an online static analysis engine that is both fast and extensible, aimed at preventing the generation of insecure or dangerous code by coding agents.
candidates.primary_claim: Real-time multi-layered defense framework with three guardrails (PromptGuard 2, Agent Alignment Checks, CodeShield) serving as final layer of defense for LLM-powered agents.
candidates.delta_layer_mapping: delta2 (PromptGuard 2 + Agent Alignment Checks) + delta3 (CodeShield static analysis + extensible safety policies)
candidates.critical_gap_vs_aegis: Pas de specialisation medicale/chirurgicale, pas d'AllowedOutputSpec formelle au sens dataclass Python avec contraintes biomechaniques FDA-ancrees (tension 50-800g, freeze_instruments, HL7 OBX directives).

candidates.title: Unregulated large language models produce medical device-like output
candidates.institution: University of Pennsylvania Perelman School of Medicine
candidates.abstract_verbatim: Large language models (LLMs) show considerable promise for clinical decision support (CDS) but none is currently authorized by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) as a CDS device. We evaluated whether two popular LLMs could be induced to provide device-like CDS output. We found that LLM output readily produced device-like decision support across a range of scenarios, suggesting a need for regulation if LLMs are formally deployed for clinical use.
candidates.primary_claim: Unregulated LLMs produce FDA device-like clinical decision support output despite disclaimers; new methods beyond prompts are needed to constrain LLM output for regulatory compliance.
candidates.delta_layer_mapping: Justifie le BESOIN de delta3 dans le domaine medical
candidates.relevance_to_claim: MEDIUM-HIGH (contextualise le besoin, pas la solution)
candidates.critical_insight_for_aegis: Citation directe : 'effective regulation may require new methods to better constrain LLM output, and prompts are inadequate for this purpose' — renforce la justification de validate_output/AllowedOutputSpec comme reponse a un besoin documente publiquement.

candidates.title: Guardrails AI: Python framework for reliable AI applications (output validation via composable validators)
candidates.venue: GitHub open-source (pas de paper academique dedie)
candidates.abstract_verbatim: Guardrails is a Python framework that helps build reliable AI applications by performing input/output validation and generating structured LLM data. Uses Pydantic integration for schema definition with composable validators (pre-built or custom) and OnFailAction enums to define failure responses.
candidates.primary_claim: Framework Python generique pour validation input/output via validators composables + schemas Pydantic + OnFailAction enums.
candidates.delta_layer_mapping: delta3 (output validation via Pydantic schemas mais SANS specification formelle des contraintes metier)
candidates.critical_gap_vs_aegis: Pydantic schemas sont declaratifs structurels (types et shapes), PAS des contraintes semantiques metier (max_tension_g, forbidden_tools). Pas de validation contextuelle Allowed(i).

candidates.title: LLM Guard: Comprehensive security scanner for LLM inputs/outputs
candidates.abstract_verbatim: Comprehensive tool to fortify the security of LLMs with 15 prompt scanners and 21 output scanners for sanitization, detection of harmful language, prevention of data leakage, and resistance against prompt injection attacks.
candidates.primary_claim: Toolkit multi-scanner (36 detecteurs au total) pour securite input/output LLM.
candidates.critical_gap_vs_aegis: Architecture scanners paralleles sans specification centralisee. Pas de notion d'Allowed(i) contextuelle.

candidates.title: Prompting Is Programming: A Query Language for Large Language Models (LMQL)
candidates.venue: PLDI 2023 (ACM SIGPLAN Conference on Programming Language Design and Implementation)
candidates.abstract_verbatim: Large language models have demonstrated outstanding performance on a wide range of tasks such as question answering and code generation. On a high level, given an input, a language model can be used to automatically complete the sequence in a statistically-likely way. Based on this, users prompt these models with language instructions or examples, to implement a variety of downstream tasks. Advanced prompting methods can even imply interaction between the language model, a user, and external tools such as calculators. However, to obtain state-of-the-art performance or adapt language models for specific tasks, complex task- and model-specific programs have to be implemented, which may still require ad-hoc interaction. Based on this, we present the novel idea of Language Model Programming (LMP). LMP generalizes language model prompting from pure text prompts to an intuitive combination of text prompting and scripting. Additionally, LMP allows constraints to be specified over the language model output. This enables easy adaption to many tasks while abstracting language model internals and providing high-level semantics.
candidates.primary_claim: DSL (Language Model Programming) combinant text prompting et scripting avec contraintes sur les outputs.
candidates.validation_pattern_exact: where clauses : 'where len(ANSWER) < 120 and STOPS_AT(ANSWER, ".")' ; typed vars : '[NUM: int]'
candidates.delta_layer_mapping: delta3 (constraints LLM output) mais via DSL inline et pas specification object
candidates.noteworthy: Meme premier auteur (Beurer-Kellner) que P126 Tramer et al. 2025 'Design Patterns for Securing LLM Agents against Prompt Injections' -- continuite de recherche sur la separation formelle.
candidates.critical_gap_vs_aegis: Contraintes DSL inline (format + type), PAS specification formelle semantique metier avec Reachable/Allowed formel.

preliminary_verdict.claim_raw: quatrieme implementation delta3 via validate_output + AllowedOutputSpec
main_issues: Le pattern generique (validate output contre specification) existe deja : Guardrails AI 2023, LMQL 2022, LlamaFirewall 2025. AEGIS n'est PAS la 4eme implementation d'un pattern, c'est plutot la 6eme ou 7eme.

La vraie contribution originale est la SPECIALISATION : AllowedOutputSpec encode des contraintes biomecaniques ancrees FDA pour robot chirurgical Da Vinci Xi (max_tension_g, forbidden_tools chirurgicaux, directives HL7 OBX). Aucun autre framework identifie ne fait cette specialisation medicale formelle.

npj Digital Medicine 2025 (Weissman et al.) confirme publiquement que de nouvelles methodes 'beyond prompts' sont necessaires pour contraindre les outputs LLM en contexte medical FDA. AEGIS repond a ce besoin identifie.
preliminary_verdict.proposed_rewording_short: Premiere implementation delta3 specialisee medicale chirurgicale, etendant le pattern output validation (Guardrails AI, LMQL, LlamaFirewall) avec des contraintes biomecaniques formelles ancrees FDA 510k pour le robot Da Vinci Xi.
cls_to_pass_to_analyst: LlamaFirewall (P131) -- comparaison ligne a ligne avec validate_output AEGIS
npj DM (P132) -- citation pour la justification
LMQL (P135) -- ancrage historique (meme auteur que P126 Tramer)
